items.verse: فَتَبَسَّمَ ضَاحِكًۭا مِّن قَوْلِهَا وَقَالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِىٓ أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَىَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَىَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَـٰلِحًۭا تَرْضَىٰهُ وَأَدْخِلْنِى بِرَحْمَتِكَ فِى عِبَادِكَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ
items.verseEn: So [Solomon] smiled, amused at her speech, and said, "My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me by Your mercy into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants."

items.verse: وَٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ قَآئِمَةٌۭ فَضَحِكَتْ فَبَشَّرْنَـٰهَا بِإِسْحَـٰقَ وَمِن وَرَآءِ إِسْحَـٰقَ يَعْقُوبَ
items.verseEn: And his wife was standing, and she smiled. Then We gave her good tidings of Isaac and after Isaac, Jacob.
He [i.e., the Prophet (ﷺ) ] frowned and turned away
And they fall upon their faces weeping, and it [i.e., the Qur’ān] increases them in humble submission.

items.en: The thin membrane of a date seed
items.verse: يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ كُلٌّۭ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍۢ مُّسَمًّۭى ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِن قِطْمِيرٍ
items.verseEn: He causes the night to enter the day, and He causes the day to enter the night and has subjected the sun and the moon - each running [its course] for a specified term. That is Allāh, your Lord; to Him belongs sovereignty. And those whom you invoke other than Him do not possess [as much as] the membrane of a date seed.

items.en: The speck on a date seed
items.verse: وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌۭ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ نَقِيرًۭا
items.verseEn: And whoever does righteous deeds, whether male or female, while being a believer - those will enter Paradise and will not be wronged, [even as much as] the speck on a date seed.

items.en: The thread in a date seed's groove
items.verse: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يُزَكُّونَ أَنفُسَهُم ۚ بَلِ ٱللَّهُ يُزَكِّى مَن يَشَآءُ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلًا
items.verseEn: Have you not seen those who claim themselves to be pure? Rather, Allāh purifies whom He wills, and injustice is not done to them, [even] as much as a thread [inside a date seed].

items.verse: وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ كَسَرَابٍۭ بِقِيعَةٍۢ يَحْسَبُهُ ٱلظَّمْـَٔانُ مَآءً حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَهُۥ لَمْ يَجِدْهُ شَيْـًۭٔا وَوَجَدَ ٱللَّهَ عِندَهُۥ فَوَفَّىٰهُ حِسَابَهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ
items.verseEn: But those who disbelieved - their deeds are like a mirage in a lowland which a thirsty one thinks is water until, when he comes to it, he finds it is nothing but finds Allāh before him, and He will pay him in full his due; and Allāh is swift in account.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مَمْنُوعَةٌ مِنَ الصَّرْفِ
categories.nameEn: Words that are grammatically diptotes (al-mamnu' min al-sarf)

items.verse: وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مَرْيَمَ إِذِ ٱنتَبَذَتْ مِنْ أَهْلِهَا مَكَانًۭا شَرْقِيًّۭا
items.verseEn: And mention, [O Muḥammad], in the Book [the story of] Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.

items.verse: أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَآءَ إِذْ حَضَرَ يَعْقُوبَ ٱلْمَوْتُ إِذْ قَالَ لِبَنِيهِ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِنۢ بَعْدِى قَالُوا۟ نَعْبُدُ إِلَـٰهَكَ وَإِلَـٰهَ ءَابَآئِكَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ إِلَـٰهًۭا وَٰحِدًۭا وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُسْلِمُونَ
items.verseEn: Or were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, when he said to his sons, "What will you worship after me?" They said, "We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac - one God. And we are Muslims [in submission] to Him."

items.verse: نَحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ أَحْسَنَ ٱلْقَصَصِ بِمَآ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ وَإِن كُنتَ مِن قَبْلِهِۦ لَمِنَ ٱلْغَـٰفِلِينَ
items.verseEn: We relate to you, [O Muḥammad], the best of stories in what We have revealed to you of this Qur’ān although you were, before it, among the unaware.

items.verse: لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَـٰفِعُ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍۢ مُّسَمًّۭى ثُمَّ مَحِلُّهَآ إِلَى ٱلْبَيْتِ ٱلْعَتِيقِ
items.verseEn: For you therein [i.e., the animals marked for sacrifice] are benefits for a specified term; then their place of sacrifice is at the ancient House.

categories.name: أَسْمَاءُ عُيُونٍ وَأَنْهَارٍ مُحَدَّدَةٍ
categories.nameEn: Names of specific springs or rivers

items.verse: وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ ۖ فَإِذَا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ فَأَلْقِيهِ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ وَلَا تَخَافِى وَلَا تَحْزَنِىٓ ۖ إِنَّا رَآدُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ وَجَاعِلُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
items.verseEn: And We inspired to the mother of Moses, "Suckle him; but when you fear for him, cast him into the river and do not fear and do not grieve. Indeed, We will return him to you and will make him [one] of the messengers."
And its mixture is of Tasneem,
[From] a fountain within it [i.e., Paradise] named Salsabeel.
Indeed, We have granted you, [O Muḥammad], al-Kawthar.

harf.hint: A guide in the darkness of the desert and sea.
harf.verse: 53:1 — By the star when it descends,

deduction.title: The Carrier of Spiteful Fuel
deduction.intro: In the shadow of a close relation to a prophet, this soul chose darkness over light. Driven by malice, their actions were not merely words of scorn but deeds intended to inflict physical harm, earning a unique and terrible mention in a chapter of the Quran. Who is this figure, forever linked to a fire of their own stoking?
clues: I was connected by marriage to one of the most powerful families in my city.
My spouse was a direct relative of a prophet, yet we were among his most bitter enemies.
I actively participated in the persecution, not content to let my spouse act alone.
I would gather thorny wood and scatter it in the path the prophet would walk at night.
A whole chapter of the Quran was revealed condemning my spouse and me, mentioning us both.
Allah describes me specifically as the 'carrier of firewood' with a rope of palm-fiber around my neck.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Wife of Fir'awn
The Wife of Prophet Lut
The Wife of Abu Lahab
The Wife of Al-'Aziz
Hind bint 'Utbah

trial.label: What Was My Deed?
options: Betraying guests to a wicked town
Scattering thorny wood to harm the Prophet (ﷺ)
Attempting to seduce a righteous servant
Spreading slander against a noble woman
Persuading my spouse to build a tall tower
trial.answer: Scattering thorny wood to harm the Prophet (ﷺ)

location.label: What Was Mentioned With Me?
options: A tray of heavenly food
A staff that turned into a serpent
Garments of pitch
A rope of palm-fiber
A necklace of great value

outcome.label: What Was My Fate?
options: Drowning in the sea with an army
Being swallowed by the earth
Repenting and being accepted into Islam
Being specifically condemned in the Quran to a blazing fire
Being turned into a pillar of salt
outcome.answer: Being specifically condemned in the Quran to a blazing fire

deduction.verse: And his wife [as well] - the carrier of firewood. (111:4)

scramble.hint: This verse states the ultimate purpose for the creation of both jinn and humankind.
scramble.arabic: وَمَا خَلَقْتُ ٱلْجِنَّ وَٱلْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ
scramble.verseEn: And I did not create the jinn and mankind except to worship Me.

verse.arabic_text: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا
verse.translation: O you who have believed, fear Allah and speak words of appropriate justice.

theme_question.correct: The importance of combining God-consciousness with truthful and upright speech.
options: The importance of combining God-consciousness with truthful and upright speech.
The obligation of giving charity to the poor.
The prohibition of consuming interest (riba).
The detailed rules of inheritance.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse appears in the latter part of Surah Al-Ahzab, which provides social and ethical guidance to the nascent Muslim community in Medina. It serves as a general, powerful principle for all believers, linking inner piety directly to the integrity of one's speech as a cornerstone of a righteous life.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The core theme is the inseparable link between God-consciousness (Taqwa) and righteous speech. 'Qawlan Sadida' refers to speech that is truthful, just, direct, and free from falsehood or frivolity. The verse teaches that rectifying one's speech is a means to rectifying all of one's actions and attaining Allah's forgiveness, highlighting the profound impact our words have on our spiritual state.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-Ahzab ('The Combined Forces') is named after the Battle of the Trench, where a confederation of tribes besieged Medina. The surah recounts this event, highlighting the believers' trust in God. It also establishes crucial social legislation for the Muslim community, including rulings on adoption, divorce, the special status of the Prophet's wives (Mothers of the Believers), and the observance of hijab, thereby shaping the community's identity and conduct.
